risks: shared .git/objects store across worktrees (RR; path policy blocks .git)
capabilities.notes: Isolated git linked-worktrees outside the base repo; protected-branch refusal; symlink-escape and git-hook neutralization verified against real fixtures.

risks: WSL2 is not a sufficient sandbox by itself (DoD §6 invariant 13)
no OS-level syscall/network namespace confinement in 1.0 (RR-4 residual; future hardening)
capabilities.notes: A10.2 real isolation boundary verified by a 13-invariant fixture matrix: traversal, symlink/hardlink escape, /mnt/c escape, $HOME escape, credential-path denial, env credential-leak denial, .gitattributes filter neutralization (T-FS-05), network/destructive/privileged deny-by-default, .git protection, declared limits. In-process policy composition is the mechanism (ADR 0055); WSL2 is not trusted as the boundary.
Allowlist containment: traversal, symlink-escape, hardlink-clobber, absolute-path, cross-worktree, .git all denied; maxFiles budget enforced.
Deny-by-default command classification; shell disabled (metacharacters inert); destructive/privileged/network denied by default; denied command never spawns.
Timeout, output byte cap, and process-group cancellation (SIGTERM→SIGKILL on POSIX; taskkill /T on Windows).
Cancellation of a REAL provider process group. Mock-verified today; real pending the auth step.
Hash-chained, tamper-evident, secret-redacted file-mutation ledger; reconciliation detects out-of-band changes.
Structured gate results from real exit codes, bound to the tested diff hash.
Bounded repair loop (rounds/wall-time/commands/files/quota) with terminal states; mock-driven verification.
Re-derived verdict; decision binding to diff/ledger/gate hashes prevents replay and post-decision modification.
Capacity never fabricated (unknown stays null); reserves protect critical phases; hard stop works without a balance signal; isBillingAuthoritative always false.
